title: Coeloglossum cordatum
taxonomy: Coeloglossum cordatum is classified in the kingdom Plantae, with the specific classification of Orchidaceae as its family and Orchidoideae as its subfamily. It belongs to the genus Coeloglossum. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants. Within the Orchidaceae family, Coeloglossum cordatum is one of the species that can be found. Its classification helps in understanding its growth habits and requirements for survival.
uses: Coeloglossum cordatum, also known as the summer lady's tresses, is a type of orchid with potential medicinal uses. While more research is needed, it has been studied for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Coeloglossum cordatum, also known as the green wood orchid, is a type of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae.
